entry.title: Re: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content: Nice blog. But you skipped the tenant isolation in the database, which is not really ensured with the service manager and a service-instance schema. This schema cannot be managed by the database independently from other schemas. That is a real gap in your solution. The right way is using the HANA Cloud native multitenancy capability, with customer-specific encrypted database tenants (containing schemas or HDI containers), resource control and independent lifecycle.   For native database tenants you use the Tenant Management Service instead of the Service Manager. See here for more details: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy/sap-hana-cloud-multitenancy?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: How to make a field mandatory or warning trigger for...
entry.content:
Dear @jerrysam,

Option A: Recommended

Use Cloud Badi MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD (Check Invoice Data) to raise a W (warning) or E (error) message when the header KSeF field is empty. This is the same mechanism SAP recommends to enforce supplier-invoice checks in Public Cloud

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3673139

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/5a27c5f57c3f4dffa6881ca587b22571.html

Steps to do:

Option A:

1. Make sure the field exists on the invoice header:

  • If you already have a standard KSeF reference number/KSeF field available for supplier invoices use it SAP provides configuration/support for maintaining KSeF reference number in supplier invoices
  • If you don’t have it, create a custom field in Custom Fields and Logic with business context publish it and add it to the UI key user extensibility / Adapt UI. Available fields you can find in Badi below:

 

2. Go to Custom Logic

  • Create a new implementation for Badi: MRM_CHECK_INVOICE_CLOUD

 

3. Implement the check

  • Use the ACTION parameter to decide when to show/stop:
    • Use action CHECK if you want users to see the message while entering the invoice when they press Check/Simulate
    • Use action POST / SAVE AS COMPLETED if you want to block posting when KSeF is missing
  • If the KSeF field is initial/blank append a message to the provided MESSAGES table:
    • Warning: message type W (allows posting, but warns)
    • Error: message type E (prevents posting)

For example:

    " Example 4: Issue an warning message

    " Only issue the message if YOUR FIELD is not filled

    " This message will not prevent any actions, however it will be displayed to the user

    " Please note that warning, success and information messages will only be displayed during the check action

    IF headerdata-YOURFIELD IS INITIAL AND

       action = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_action_check.

      APPEND VALUE #( messagetype = if_ex_mrm_check_invoice_cloud=>c_messagetype_warning

                     messageid = 'MRM_BADI_CLOUD'

                     messagenumber = '001'

                     messagevariable1 = YOUR FIELD is not filled.'  ) TO messages.

 

    ENDIF.

More details you will find inside Badi clicking:

 

4. Activate the implementation and test in Create Supplier Invoice Advanced (MIRO)

Option B:

If SAP already issues a standard message for your scenario and you only want to switch it between I/W/E you can use in CBC ID 102091 Define Message Types for Area M8

 

SAP Note below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2654157

IMPORTANT: You cannot change SAP predefined standard message type entries only customer-specific settings are possible and some changes are restricted by design so in my opinion option A is suitable for your case

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: GL Account not available as Preconditition for Market...
entry.content:
Good day @JavierIbanez 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is not a “forgotten field”. In Manage Solution and Validation Rules, the available precondition fields are restricted based on the business context. Market Segment context derives or validates profitability segment fields and only some drivers are exposed. See Manage Substitution/Validation Rules and KBA 2892205 for more details.

So, it is rather a scope and field exposure limitation of that specific business context in the key user app.

Regarding the availability of GL Account being available in Custom Logic BAdI, this often happens because Runtime structure has more fields and key user rule UI exposes only stable subset. That is based on the clean core key user tools design.

  • If your substitution must be driven by GL account, the clean approach I recommend is to keep the Market Segment rule simple or skip the rule app for this case. Implement the condition and substitution in FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB. This will help you avoid creating and populating a ZZ_GLACCOUNT like workaround field.
  • Alternatively, if your real need is GL account-based substitution/validation during posting, then check whether your scenario can be solved with a Journal Entry Item type context where GL Account is exposed. You would then substitute fields that are available in that context, not necessarily market segment extension fields.

Regarding raising an incident, if your expectation is for SAP to expose GL Account as a selectable precondition in Market Segment context, then that would rather be an enhancement request and not an incident. KBA 2892205 also addresses this boundary.

Did you also consider  GL Account Hierarchies ?

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
Hi MioYasutake

This is a really good question. And it highlights the most common problem with every human-in-the-loop scenario I have seen so far: I have not seen one of them that is a loop!

They are a human in a serial approval (Yes/No) step after the AI. There is:

  • very little reasoning showing how the AI came to its conclusion (this is a huge problem because mostly the AI doesn't know so it can't say)
  • very little the human can do beyond approve or reject
  • no path for the human to feed their reasoning back to the AI so that the AI learns better the context of its recommendations and therefore to make better recommendations.

In other words, it is not a loop. The term is mostly a comfort blanket for marketing purposes, so people (customers) will trust the decisions made behind boundaries they cannot see through.

It is covering one bad situation by making another one on top of it.

This is not to say that there are no loops - just that I haven't seen any yet.

If we want to have entry-level people in our business who can learn and perform to the standard we aspire to, we have to provide them with the information context to learn and improve. We have to build real actual functioning loops, and not talk about them or mis-label things that are not loops.

Hope that helps to answer your question.

Cheers
Denver

entry.title: ❗ Unable to Deploy MDK Project to Cloud Foundry – html5-push Fails (Service Instance Not Found)
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I’m facing an issue while deploying my MDK project to SAP BTP Cloud Foundry. The deployment fails during the html5-push step with the following error:

Executing task: node /extbin/local/openvscode-server/extensions/mdk-vsc-wing-25.9.12/res/scripts/deployweb.js {"application":{"name":"CDPLIndent","basePath":"/home/user/projects/CDPLIndent_Web","fullPath":"/home/user/projects/CDPLIndent_Web/Application.app","workspacePath":"/home/user/projects"}} 

MTA Build path:/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory
start web deploy...
cf env nsCDPLIndentQA-approuter | grep SAP_ROUTER_VERSION
SAP_ROUTER_VERSION: 21

cd "/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory/MDKModule" && cf html5-push -n nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host
FAILED
Service instance with name 'nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host' not found

Command failed: cd "/home/user/projects/WebApplicationFactory/MDKModule" && cf html5-push -n nsCDPLIndentQA_html_repo_host

🧩 What I have already checked:

  • The CF CLI is logged into the correct subaccount and space.
  • The approuter app (nsCDPLIndentQA-approuter) is deployed and running.
Has anyone faced this specific Service instance not found error during MDK deployment?
What was the fix?

📌 Additional Info

  • MDK plugin version: 25.9.12
  • Deployment target: Cloud Foundry (SAP BTP)

Any guidance or similar experiences will be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
@qmacro 

I completely understand. I've also noticed that the team is too small to complete all the necessary tasks.
You're not the first to suggest this, either: Jerry has already written something about it.
Specifying what should and shouldn't be reported is a sensible short-term solution, but in the long term, more resources are needed.
In the long term, the problem simply has to be solved by allocating more resources. This brings me back to the point that the SAP community is not receiving the strategic support it should be. This leaves me feeling pessimistic, because the community is so important, yet so chronically neglected that it simply doesn't allow for any major leaps forward.
